name: bibt-gcp-asset

metadata.name: bibt-gcp-asset
metadata.summary: Functionality often used by BITS Blue Team (GCP Asset Module).

documentation:
# bibt-gcp-asset: BITS Blue Team Utilities: GCP Asset API Module

- **Developer**: Matthew OBrien
- **Email**: mobrien@broadinstitute.org
- **Project URL**: https://pypi.org/project/bibt-gcp-asset/
- **Project Repo**: https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-asset
- **Project Documentation**: https://bibt-gcp-asset.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## Installing

```bash
$ pip install --upgrade bibt-gcp-asset
```

## Usage

- See documentation here: https://bibt-gcp-asset.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## All BIBT Libraries

- [bibt-gcp-asset](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-asset)
- [bibt-gcp-bq](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-bq)
- [bibt-gcp-iam](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-iam)
- [bibt-gcp-pubsub](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-pubsub)
- [bibt-gcp-scc](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-scc)
- [bibt-gcp-secrets](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-secrets)
- [bibt-gcp-storage](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-storage)
- [bibt-qualys](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-qualys)
- [bibt-sentinelone](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-sentinelone)
- [bibt-slack](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-slack)

classes.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client.list_assets
documentation:
List assets from the CAI API. For more information, view

: [the documentation](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/reference/rest/v1/assets/list).

```
type parent



param parent

the parent resource to search under. Can be one of:
organizations/1234, folders/1234, projects/1234,
or projects/name.

type asset_types



param asset_types

a list of asset types to return, in the format
compute.googleapis.com/Disk (for example). See
here
for all supported types.

type content_type



param content_type

the type of data to return. usually you'll want
RESOURCE, but can be any value from
here.

type page_size



param page_size

the number of results to return per page. a lower number will
result in more API calls.

rtype

ListAssetsPager

returns

a pager which may be iterated on to retrieve results. contains
Asset objects.
```

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client.get_asset
documentation:
Get a specific asset by name from the CAI API.

```
type scope



param scope

the parent resource to search under. Can be one of: organizations/1234,
folders/1234, projects/1234, or projects/name.

type asset_name



param asset_name

the name of the asset, in the form of //cloudsql.googleapis.com/projects/my-project/instances/my-db.

type asset_types



param asset_types

a list of asset types to return, in the format compute.googleapis.com/Disk (for example).
See here
for all supported types.

type detailed



param detailed

if true, will get the full resource metadata from a list_assets call, otherwise
just returns basic metadata from search_assets.

type page_size



param page_size

the number of results to return per page. a lower number will
result in more API calls.

rtype

Asset

returns

an asset object (or None).
```

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client.get_parent_project
documentation:
For a given scope and asset, attempts to retrieve the parent project. If a project, folder,
or organization is passed, simply returns that.

```
type scope



param scope

the parent resource to search under. Can be one of: organizations/1234,
folders/1234, projects/1234, or projects/name.

type asset

Asset

param asset

the asset object for which to return the parent.

rtype

Asset

returns

an asset object representing a project, folder, or organization (or None).
```

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client.search_assets
documentation:
Search assets from the CAI API. This provides minimal asset metadata, use `list_assets` for full information.
For more information, view [the documentation](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/searching-resources#search_resources).

```
type scope



param scope

the parent resource to search under. Can be one of: organizations/1234,
folders/1234, projects/1234, or projects/name.

type query



param query

an asset query, see here
for more information.

type asset_types



param asset_types

a list of asset types to return, in the format compute.googleapis.com/Disk (for example).
See here
for all supported types.

type order_by



param order_by

the field(s) to order results by. sorting can increase response wait time. view the documentation linked
above for usable fields.

type page_size



param page_size

the number of results to return per page. a lower number will result in more API calls.

rtype

SearchAllResourcesPager

returns

a pager which may be iterated on to retrieve results. contains
ResourceSearchResult objects.
```

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client.search_asset_iam_policy
documentation:
Search IAM policies from the CAI API.
For more information, view [the documentation](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/searching-iam-policies#search_policies).

```
type scope



param scope

the parent resource to search under. Can be one of: organizations/1234,
folders/1234, projects/1234, or projects/name.

type query



param query

an asset query, see here
for more information.

rtype

SearchAllIamPoliciesPager

returns

a pager which may be iterated on to retrieve results. contains
IamPolicySearchResult objects.
```

methods.name: bibt.gcp.asset.classes.Client._generate_asset_search_str
documentation:
Generates a query string for `get_asset` based
on whether or not a project ID is passed.

Instantiates a Client object for further API calls.

```python
from bibt.gcp import asset

client = asset.Client(os.environ["GCP_ORG_ID"])
asset = client.get_asset(...)
```

```
type gcp_org_id



param gcp_org_id

your GCP organization ID.
needed to query the Cloud Asset Inventory API.

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making API calls, if not
using the account running the function for authentication.
```
